name: Squid Stuffed with Ricotta and Spinach
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Fresh spinach, trimmed of stems and washed, or 1/2 package frozen thawed
Coarse salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
Squid Stuffed With Ricotta And Spinach
Do not overstuff the squid or they may split while they cook. Rice and braised fennel go well with this dish.
1. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit. Clean the squids and chop the tentacles finely. Set them aside.
2. Sauté the onion and the garlic in 1 tablespoon of olive oil and butter until the onion is soft. Add the tentacles and cook for 2 minutes. Add the spinach and sauté, stirring, until it has wilted. Drain off any extra liquid and cool the spinach.
3. In a mixing bowl, combine the ricotta, egg, parsley, and the cooled spinach mixture. Mix thoroughly and season with salt, pepper, and hot pepper flakes.
4. Stuff the mixture loosely into the squids and close the openings securely with a toothpick.
5. Use the remaining tablespoon of olive oil to grease a rectangular baking dish large enough to hold the squids comfortably in one layer. Arrange the squids in the dish and add the wine and tomatoes. Season with salt, pepper and more hot pepper flakes, if you wish.
6. Bake for 40 to 45 minutes, or until the squids are tender and the sauce has thickened. If there is too much sauce, raise the oven temperature and allow the liquid to reduce. If there is too little, add more white wine. Serve the squids with lemon quarters.
Yield: 4 servings.
